rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical aerobic functionalization of styrenes using O2, where Pt anode gives oxidative C=C cleavage to carbonyls and carbon anode gives oxidative dimerization to 2,5-diaryl tetrahydrofurans.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of styrene to its radical cation; cathodic single-electron reduction of O2 to superoxide (O2•-); electrode material dictates which species (O2•- vs neutral styrene) traps the styrene radical cation.

key_intermediates.name: styrene oxide / epoxide (2e, minor path for cleavage)
formaldehyde (HCHO, cleavage co-product detected by GC-MS)

electron_flow: At Pt anode, styrene is oxidized to a radical cation that is trapped by cathodically generated O2•- to give a dioxetane (major) or epoxide (minor), which fragments to aryl ketone + HCHO. At carbon anode, O2•- adsorption is poor, so the styrene radical cation is intercepted by a second neutral styrene to form a distonic dimer cation, which then combines with O2•- and cyclizes to the 2,5-diaryl THF.

probe_reagents.name: 18O2 isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into both the ketone (81%) and the THF (93%, cis/trans 1:1.1), confirming O2 as the oxygen source in both reactions

probe_reagents.name: isolated epoxide 2e as substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of 2e gave only 88% acetophenone with incomplete conversion, indicating epoxide is a minor (not main) intermediate on the cleavage path

probe_reagents.name: GC-MS detection of HCHO
probe_reagents.observation: Formaldehyde detected, consistent with dioxetane fragmentation

probe_reagents.name: DFT (B3LYP/6-31G+(d,p)) spin density
probe_reagents.observation: Highest spin density of styrene radical cation localized at β-position, rationalizing β-attack by O2•- or by neutral styrene

notes: Authors do not rule out that on Pt the electrode itself adsorbs O2/O2•- and thereby delivers it to the radical cation; a radical–radical coupling pathway is also considered possible for the THF-forming step. Selectivity is explained by Pt favoring O2•- capture of the radical cation while carbon anode favors capture by neutral styrene due to raised HOMO of adsorbed styrene.
